summary: A small single-story rectangular building with a recessed dark roof center, raised stonebrick perimeter platform, oak-plank walls with vertical log corner posts, a continuous band of windows on the visible long sides, and layered trim of stone and dark nether bricks near the roofline.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with central recessed roof area (courtyard-like shallow pit)
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along long axis (appears symmetric on both long sides)

elements: continuous row of 3-4 glass windows on each long side set in oak plank wall
stonebrick raised border/platform around the building one block high
vertical log corner posts (two visible on long side)
layered trim near roof: stone slab band then dark nether-brick band then stone slab cap
central recessed roof area filled with dark blocks (nether_brick) below the slab cap
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular oak-plank box ~9x9 interior with vertical log posts at each corner.
Place a one-block-high stonebrick border platform around the exterior footprint extending one block out.
Add a horizontal band: stone slab layer directly above windows, then 1 block tall nether brick layer, then stone slab cap around the top edge.
Inset a recessed central roof area two blocks inside the slab cap and fill it with nether_brick flat surface.
Windows: place contiguous glass panes/windows (3 or 4) along the long walls between log posts at head-height.
uncertainties: Exact block types: stone slab vs stone brick slab are inferred from appearance; named as stone_slab and stonebrick with high but not certain confidence.
Exact internal layout and presence of an entrance door: no clear door visible on shown sides; entrance location uncertain (possibly on short side not shown).
Precise footprint dimensions: estimated from visible block counts and top-down images; could be off by a couple of blocks.
Roof depth: recessed center depth and whether central area is open to sky or covered with dark blocks inferred from top views but not fully certain.
